response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with a long track record at major companies, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, Linux kernel source-code modification experience, and deep L2/L3 networking and security-protocol knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal) — none of which are evidenced. His C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), his listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German, and his embedded experience is limited to peripheral work with robotic arms. The core stack throughout his career is web technologies (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang), which this firmware role does not use.
strengths: Strong, sustained senior-level engineering career including staff-level roles at large tech companies
Solid Linux, Docker, and CI/CD infrastructure experience with a testing-focused engineering culture
Some C/C++ background (LabWindows/CVI components, C++ audio thesis) and demonstrated low-latency systems work
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native level)
No embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP, NETCONF, L2/L3)
No Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience; C proficiency is minimal and over a decade old

key_factors.factor: No embedded security/networking domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, kernel work)
Strong general software engineering seniority and Linux background
